LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SHREVE & CO. JEWELERS, LTD.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - AS TO COUNT 1, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF COMMERCIAL CODE 2725 IS CONTRARY TO THE STATUTE, WHICH PROVIDES: "A CAUSE OF ACTION ACCRUES WHEN THE BREACH OCCURS, REGARDLESS OF THE AGGRIEVED PARTY'S LACK OF KNOWLEDGE OF THE BREACH." AS TO COUNTS 2 AND 3, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. FALSE ADVERTISING AND FALSE REPRESENTATIONS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED, BEYOND CONTRACT BREACH. AS TO COUNT 4, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PENAL CODE 496 REGARDS RECEIPT OF STOLEN PROPERTY, WHICH IS NOT ALLEGED HERE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189 =302/RU

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-03-2018 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Set For Trial On May-21-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHREVE & CO. JEWELERS, LTD.'s, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Shreve & Co. Jewelers, Ltd.'s demurrer to the third cause of action in the first amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiff Jane Mudge's claim for violation of Penal Code 496(c) is adequately alleged and the face of the first amended complaint does not establish that the claim is time-barred. Judge Kahn, who is deciding this demurrer, disagrees with the apparent position of Judge Ulmer as expressed in the June 16, 2017 order. The prior order states that "Penal Code Sec. 496 regards receipt of stolen property, which is not alleged here," which suggests that only an allegation of receipt of stolen property suffices to state a section 496 claim. However, Bell v. Feibush (2013) 212 Cal.App.4th 1041 holds that an allegation that a defendant received money under false pretenses is sufficient to state a section 496 claim. Ms. Mudge has makes such an allegation. Ms. Mudge's allegation that she didn't discover Shreve's alleged misconduct until 2016 is sufficient to preclude sustaining the demurrer on statute of limitations grounds. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2017 Continued To Oct-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-16-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. VICTYOR MENG OF GAW/POE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-766-7451 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SCOTT M. MCLEOD OF COOPER WHITE & COOPER, TEL. NO. 1-415-765-6299 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SHREVE & CO. JEWELERS, LTD.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - As to Count 1, the demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff's interpretation of Commercial Code 2725 is contrary to the statute, which provides: "A cause of action accrues when the breach occurs, regardless of the aggrieved party's lack of knowledge of the breach." As to Counts 2 and 3, the demurrer is OVERRULED. False advertising and false representations are adequately pled, beyond contract breach. As to Count 4, the demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO Penal Code 496 regards receipt of stolen property, which is not alleged here. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/ru
